agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians, with strict compliance controls around physician licensing, scheduling integrity, medical record privacy, and consultation note immutability.","bounded_context":"Appointment & Consultation Management — covers booking lifecycle, physician credential verification, medical record access control, and consultation note finalization.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot linking a patient to a licensed physician for a virtual consultation. Has states: scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A physician whose medical license number has been verified by the platform and who is therefore eligible to accept bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments overlap in time for the same physician. This is a system invariant violation."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which a patient may self-cancel."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments outside the patient cancellation window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a given appointment, and the only physician with access to that patient's medical records for that consultation."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Becomes read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"Note Lock","definition":"The irreversible transition of consultation notes to a read-only state, triggered 48 hours after appointment completion. Once locked, notes cannot be edited by anyone."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's medical license number before they are permitted to accept any bookings on the platform."},{"term":"Patient Medical Records","definition":"Protected health information visible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician for a given consultation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient can only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing an unlicensed physician to treat patients violates healthcare regulations and creates legal and patient-safety liability. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create an appointment with a physician whose license_verified status is false must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments that overlap in time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking is a physical constraint — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. Violating this corrupts the scheduling system's integrity.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two appointments with status 'scheduled' or 'in-progress' may have overlapping time ranges."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their appointment up to 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted by management (e.g., changed to 4 hours or 1 hour) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request must be accepted if current_time <= appointment_start_time - 2 hours, and rejected otherwise."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"After the patient cancellation window closes, only a staff administrator can cancel an appointment.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an access-control policy. Management could extend cancellation rights to other roles without corrupting data integrity.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request for an appointment within 2 hours of start time must be rejected unless the requesting user has the staff_administrator role."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before they can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory compliance gate. Allowing unverified physicians to be booked creates a fundamentally unsafe and non-compliant system state.","testable_assertion":"The system must enforce that physician.license_verified == true before any appointment can be created referencing that physician."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and the treating physician for that consultation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Medical record access is governed by healthcare privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA). Unauthorized access represents a data breach — a corrupt and non-compliant system state.","testable_assertion":"Any request to read a patient's medical records must be denied unless the requester is the patient or the physician assigned to an active/completed appointment with that patient."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Note immutability after the lock window is a medical record integrity requirement. If locked notes can be modified, the audit trail and clinical record are corrupted.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to modify consultation notes where current_time > appointment_end_time + 48 hours must be rejected, regardless of the requester's role."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A patient selects a physician whose license has been verified","when":"The patient submits a booking request for an available time slot","then":"The appointment is created with status 'scheduled' and both patient and physician are notified"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A patient selects a physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"The patient attempts to book an appointment","then":"The system rejects the booking and displays an error indicating the physician is not available for appointments"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician has an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A second patient attempts to book the same physician for 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The system rejects the booking and indicates a scheduling conflict"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A physician has an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A second patient attempts to book the same physician for 10:30 to 11:00","then":"The system accepts the booking because the time slots are adjacent, not overlapping"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient has a scheduled appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully and the physician's time slot is freed"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A patient has a scheduled appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the patient to contact staff support"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A staff administrator and an appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"The staff administrator requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully regardless of the cancellation window"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient with a completed appointment and their treating physician","when":"The treating physician requests access to the patient's medical records","then":"The system grants access to the records"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A physician who is NOT the treating physician for a patient","when":"That physician requests access to the patient's medical records","then":"The system denies access"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment that ended 47 hours ago","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully because the 48-hour lock window has not elapsed"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An appointment that ended 49 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The system rejects the edit and indicates the notes are locked"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An appointment that ended 49 hours ago","when":"A staff administrator attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The system rejects the edit because note locking applies to all roles"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent booking requests for the same physician and overlapping time slot arrive simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Only one booking succeeds; the other is rejected with a scheduling conflict error. This must be enforced with pessimistic locking or equivalent concurrency control at the database level."},{"scenario":"A physician's license is revoked after they already have future appointments","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged for review or cancelled by a staff administrator. The physician must be immediately blocked from accepting new bookings."},{"scenario":"A patient attempts to cancel exactly 2 hours before the appointment start time","expected_behavior":"The cancellation is accepted — the boundary condition 'up to 2 hours' includes the 2-hour mark itself (i.e., current_time <= start_time - 2h)."},{"scenario":"A treating physician begins editing consultation notes before the 48-hour window and the lock triggers while they are still editing","expected_behavior":"The save should be rejected if submitted after the lock time. The system should not allow in-progress edits to bypass the lock boundary."},{"scenario":"A patient has appointments with multiple different physicians","expected_behavior":"Each physician can only see records relevant to their own consultation with that patient, not records from other physicians' consultations."},{"scenario":"An appointment is cancelled — can the treating physician still access the patient's medical records?","expected_behavior":"Access should be revoked for cancelled appointments since no treatment relationship was established. The spec says 'treating physician' which implies an active or completed consultation."},{"scenario":"Staff administrator attempts to cancel an already-completed appointment","expected_behavior":"Cancellation should be rejected — a completed appointment represents a consultation that already occurred and cannot be retroactively cancelled."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes are created but the appointment has no clear 'ended' timestamp (e.g., no-show)","expected_behavior":"The 48-hour lock timer depends on appointment_end_time. If the appointment was never completed, the system needs a policy for when/whether notes lock (e.g., based on scheduled end time or a no-show status)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Non-compliance with physician licensing or medical record privacy could result in regulatory fines, malpractice liability, and loss of operating licenses. Double-booking wastes physician time and creates patient dissatisfaction leading to churn.","operational":"Scheduling integrity is foundational — double-booking degrades physician trust in the platform. The 48-hour note lock window requires reliable time-based automation. License verification is an operational gate that affects physician onboarding velocity.","user":"Patients depend on accurate scheduling, timely cancellation rights, and confidence that their medical records are private. Physicians depend on correct scheduling and adequate time to finalize consultation notes.","risk":"Primary risks are HIPAA or equivalent privacy regulation violations (medical record access), practicing-without-license exposure (unverified physicians), and medical record tampering (note lock bypass). Each carries severe legal and reputational consequences.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Booking reliability, cancellation flexibility, medical record privacy, and trust in the platform's compliance posture"},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Schedule accuracy, protection from double-booking, adequate note-editing window, and license verification as a barrier to entry"},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Responsible for late cancellations and managing edge cases like license revocations; need clear authority boundaries"},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"Platform must demonstrably enforce privacy, licensing, and record immutability rules to satisfy regulatory audits"},{"group":"Platform Operations","impact":"Concurrency control for bookings, reliable time-based triggers for note locking, and license verification integration with external registries"}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The spec is clear, internally consistent, and covers the major domain rules explicitly. No contradictions detected. Confidence is not higher because the spec is silent on several operational edge cases that would arise in production.","high_confidence_areas":["Physician license verification as a booking prerequisite","Double-booking prevention as a hard invariant","Patient cancellation window policy (2-hour boundary)","Staff administrator override for late cancellations","Medical record access restricted to patient and treating physician","Consultation note locking at 48 hours post-appointment"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive or exclusive (assumed inclusive — 'up to' typically includes the boundary)","Medical record access scope when a patient sees multiple physicians — whether each physician sees only their own consultation records or all records","What happens to future appointments if a physician's license is revoked","Whether cancelled appointments revoke the treating physician's record access","How note locking works for no-show or incomplete appointments with no clear end time","Whether staff administrators can override the 48-hour note lock in exceptional circumstances"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive (cancellation allowed when exactly 2 hours remain)","Adjacent time slots (e.g., 10:00-10:30 and 10:30-11:00) do not constitute an overlap","Note locking is absolute — no role can edit locked notes","Medical record visibility is scoped per consultation, not globally across all of a patient's consultations with different physicians","Appointment completion sets a definitive end timestamp used for the 48-hour lock calculation"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
